impact: app/templates/intel.html:57 — the scan-history table rows now use class b-hover-row which declares base background=var(--bg) and hover=var(--surface). The original onmouseover/out pair was effectively a no-op (it referenced an undefined var(--hover) and reset to 'transparent'). Net effect: rest state is now slightly darker than the surrounding panel, and hover blends the row INTO the panel instead of away from it — inverts the typical 'highlight on hover' affordance. Three options: (a) accept the new look as consistent with the wormhole_kills sidebar aesthetic; (b) define a new utility .b-hover-transparent with rest=transparent + hover=var(--surface) and switch intel.html to it; (c) define a global --hover token if there's a canonical hover color the design system wanted. Surfaced by round-2 code review of T-031 (commit 19f427d).
title: intel.html scan history row hover: rest-state visually darker after T-031 round 3
